recipes.name: Baked King Crab Legs with Garlic Lemon Butter Sauce
recipes.brief_description: Baked King Crab Legs with Garlic Lemon Butter Sauce is a simple recipe that uses the legs of King Crabs or Stone Crabs. These variety of crabs are found in deep cold seas and are highly priced because of their size and the rich taste of their meat. There is a legal duration to fish for King Crabs (referred to as the crab-fishing months or the crab-fishing season) and currently this lasts for 2 to 4 weeks. Another factor that contributed to the high price of this type of crab is the extreme danger that the fishermen (or crabmen) faces in the cold deep seas.

recipes.name: Inabraw Recipe (Boiled Vegetables with Fried Fish)
recipes.brief_description: Inabraw is a Filipino dish composed of different kinds of vegetables and fish. It is also known as dinengdeng. The vegetables used are usually the common veggies that can be grown in one’s Philippine backyard; these are string beans, bitter melon, tomato, okra, moringa (malunggay), jute leaves (saluyot), and eggplant. There are other vegetables that…

recipes.name: Inasal Recipe
recipes.brief_description: Marinated chicken leg quarters are grilled to perfection while being basted with a special yellowish red sauce. This Filipino grilled chicken is perfect to have with rice and pickled shredded papaya.

recipes.name: Inihaw na Bangus Recipe (Grilled Milkfish)
recipes.brief_description: Milkfish stuffed with tomato and onions and grilled to perfection. This dish is best when served with a dipping sauce composed of soy sauce and lemon or calamansi. Enjoy it with warm white rice.

recipes.brief_description: Inihaw na Isaw ng Baboy is a Filipino bizarre street food made with sliced large intestines. These are pig's large intestines

recipes.name: Inihaw na Liempo
recipes.brief_description: Inihaw na Liempo is Filipino grilled pork belly marinated in soy sauce, calamansi, and garlic, then basted with a banana ketchup glaze while grilling over charcoal. Serve it hot with spiced vinegar or toyomansi and steamed white rice.

recipes.name: Inihaw na Liempo at Pusit
recipes.brief_description: Grilled pork belly, squid, and other seafood.

recipes.name: Inihaw na Panga ng Tuna (Grilled Tuna Jaw)
recipes.brief_description: Inihaw na Panga or Grilled Tuna Jaw is a good way to make grilled tuna. This recipe requires the fish to be marinated in a tart Teriyaki sauce overnight. The basting sauce is composed of soy sauce and granulated white sugar. It is best to use a charcoal grill for this dish because it will allow you to cook the fish evenly. It will also provide a nice smoky flavor to your dish.

recipes.name: Inihaw na Pork Chop
recipes.brief_description: Inihaw na Pork Chop is translated in Filipino as grilled pork chops. Perfectly sliced pork chops with skin and fat on are marinated in soy sauce and lemon

recipes.name: Inihaw na Pusit Recipe (Grilled Squid)

recipes.name: Inihaw na Pusit with Scallion

recipes.name: Inihaw na Tilapia Recipe
recipes.brief_description: Inihaw na Tilapia or Grilled Tilapia is one of my favorite summer time dishes. There are many ways to prepare inihaw na Tilapia, this recipe will guide you on how to make grilled or inihaw na Tilapia in the simplest way – rubbed with salt and pepper and cooked directly over the grill.
ingredient_names: a dash of ground black pepper

recipes.name: Instant Pancit Canton with Vegetables
recipes.brief_description: Instant Pancit Canton maybe the staple food in the Philippines next to rice. This is not a healthy food, but people consume it because it is convenient
ingredient_names: individual packages of instant pancit canton

recipes.name: Inun unan Recipe (Fish cooked in Vinegar)
recipes.brief_description: Inun unan is a Filipino fish recipe that involves fish and vinegar along with some vegetables and spices. This inun-unan recipe is easy to follow

recipes.name: Italian Sausage Pasta Recipe
recipes.brief_description: I enjoyed eating this Italian Sausage Pasta. It is a tasty pasta dish that is not complicated to prepare. Being a fan of grilled sausage (with peppers and onion), I really liked the outcome of this pasta dish.

recipes.name: Japanese Curry Recipe

ingredient_names: salt and ground black pepper to taste

recipes.name: Java Rice Recipe

recipes.name: Java Rice with Pork chop

recipes.name: Jean's Graham Mango Float
recipes.brief_description: Graham Mango Float recipe. I would describe this dessert as a big hit during fiesta celebration. I always make this on special occasion. It's an easy recipe and most of all you don't even need to bake. Although I love baking but it's nice to have a quick and easy recipe. Most of all, this is a refreshing dessert for the whole family.

recipes.name: Jelly Roll
recipes.brief_description: A Jelly Roll is a cake made of thin sheets of sponge cake. Various types of Jellies or Jams are spread on this cake before it gets rolled-up.

recipes.name: Jollibee Style Spaghetti
recipes.brief_description: Jollibee Style Spaghetti is our version of the yummy Jollibee Spaghetti that everyone loves. The result of this recipe is delicious.

recipes.name: Kalabasa Soup with Ampalaya Leaves
recipes.brief_description: It is the time of year wherein pumpkin gets a lot of attention. This time, we will not make the traditional pumpkin soup. Instead, I will show you how to cook a very simple soup using butternut squash with a Filipino twist -- I call this Kalabasa Soup with Ampalaya Leaves.

recipes.name: Kalamay hati Recipe
recipes.brief_description: Kalamay hati is a sticky Filipino rice cake. This is made from glutinous rice flour, muscovado sugar, and coconut cream.

recipes.name: Kaldereta sa Gata
recipes.brief_description: Stewed Pork with Tomato cooked in coconut milk. This is a crossover between two popular Filipino dishes, Kaldereta and Ginataan. This mildly spicy dish is rich and delicious.

recipes.name: Kalderetang Kambing
recipes.brief_description: Kalderetang Kambing is goat meat stewed in tomato sauce. The goat meat which is known as Chevon is commonly used for this dish

recipes.name: Kalderetang Kambing (Goat Stew)
recipes.brief_description: Kalderetang Kambing is the Filipino version of Goat Stew. This delicious dish is prepared using chevon (goat meat), tomato sauce, some vegetables, and liver spread. The use of liver spread makes kaldereta different from other tomato based stews.

recipes.name: Kale and Tuna Laing Recipe
recipes.brief_description: Kale and Tuna Laing is a version of the famous laing (taro leaves cooked in coconut milk). This recipe is unique because I used kale leaves as an alternative for taro leaves. This is best for people who are abroad and don't have quick access to dried taro leaves (dahon ng gabi).

recipes.name: Kamote Cue Recipe
recipes.brief_description: Kamote Cue is deep fried sweet potatoes with a coating of caramelized brown sugar. This is a common meryenda (mid-afternoon snack) in the Philippines and can mostly be seen peddled around the busy streets.

recipes.name: Kangkong and Ground Pork with Oyster Sauce Stir Fry

recipes.name: Kangkong with Bagoong Recipe

Ingredients such as soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil, cornstarch, sugar, and water are necessary for you to be able to make your own homemade Kung Pao sauce.

recipes.name: Lemon Garlic Butter Salmon with spring salad Recipe

The batter is piped into slender strips and baked until just set, giving each piece a light crunch that melts on the tongue. These cookies are a staple at holiday gatherings and make an excellent homemade gift.

recipes.name: Lengua Estofado
recipes.brief_description: Tender beef tongue braised in a sweet and tangy sauce of diced tomatoes, white wine, soy sauce, vinegar, and olives. This Filipino-Spanish stew is a holiday favorite best served over steamed white rice.

recipes.brief_description: Tender beef tongue braised in a sweet and tangy sauce of diced tomatoes, white wine, soy sauce, vinegar, and olives. This Filipino-Spanish stew is a holiday favorite best served over steamed white rice.
